title: Line-splicing should be avoided in // comments
description: When a line of code ends with the \ (backslash) character, the newline character is deleted. The compiler considers the next line a continuation of the current line, resulting in only one logical line of code. This source code transformation, known as line-splicing, occurs during the second phase of C and C++ translation.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
// This comment continues \
   on the next line // Noncompliant: line splicing in comment

// Single line comment with continuation \
   that might confuse readers

// Avoid this pattern \\ // Noncompliant
   as it's unclear
compliant: // This is a proper single line comment

/* This is a multi-line comment
   that spans multiple lines properly */

checks.message: Line-splicing should be avoided in // comments - use /* */ for multi-line comments instead
